subject: iPhone screen rotation locked — grayed out in Control Center
description: Screen stays in portrait even when user rotates phone sideways; rotation lock icon may or may not appear in status bar.

ts_steps.title: Check orientation lock
ts_steps.action: Swipe down from top-right corner (or up from bottom on older models) to open Control Center; tap the rotation lock icon so it is not highlighted.

ts_steps.title: Test in a known-good app
ts_steps.action: Open Safari or Photos and rotate the device; some apps (like Phone or Home Screen) do not support landscape, so the lock may appear app-specific.

ts_steps.title: Restart and calibrate
ts_steps.action: Force restart the iPhone (Volume Up, Volume Down, hold Side button); if gyroscope feels wrong, open Compass app to trigger sensor recalibration.

internal_information: Orientation lock is a simple toggle but users often confuse it with mute switch on older iPads. Display Zoom mode on some models disables landscape on Home Screen. Rarely a gyroscope hardware fault.
